channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Faulty lighting at Manorway Green and off Rollesby Way, flytipping in the Rollesby Way area, 6th March
item.description:
Only half the lights are working in the park at Manorway Green. There is flytipping at Rollesby Way as well as in the Rollesby Way area as you turn left from Rollesby Way. There is also a defective street light on the corner of Rollesby Way when turning off Rollesby Way to the right.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rollesby Way, Thamesmead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement bollard required, 6th March
item.description:
We recently had a tree removed from the grass verge outside our property number 37 Oakwood Drive, Barnehurst DA7 6EG. No replacement tree is currently planned by the council. We are currently experiencing vehicles constantly parking on the verge causing severe mud baths and churning up the grass, ruining it and making it look very unpleasant. In our road we have a school entrance and there is always heavy traffic volumes, all competing to get as close to the entrance as possible and driving up and over the grass verge and churning it up. In our neighbouring road, Inglewood Road, we notice that bollards have been installed on the grass verges in several places to prevent vehicles mounting the verges and we would like to request a bollard to be placed on the grass verge outside our property to prevent cars, delivery drivers etc from ruining the verge any more. Please see attached photos of the verge. I am happy to discuss this matter with someone : you can ring me on 07709706976 If there is a particular form I need to fill in, in order to progress my application then please advise me accordingly. Thanking you in advance for your assistance with this matter.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oakwood Drive, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumping - overflowing litter bins, 6th March
item.description:
Residents of 13 Leonard Robbins Path dupmed unsorted rubbish in they bins and then into neighbour's ones. Caught them dumping into my bins, too.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Leonard Robbins Path, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Toilet dumped in street, 6th March
item.description:
Toilet dumped outside house getting refurb (not sure if come from there). Very obviously in the street and taking up footpath.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, Whateley Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ongoing problem with blocked gulley. Two attempts have been made within tje last 2 months but still blocked, 6th March
item.description:
Overflows from the smallest amount of rain, causing waves as vehicles pass which is a danger to passers by. It is also causing irrepairable damage to my driveway. Debris from the waves of water narrowly miss my car too
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Edison Grove, Plumstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned van, again, 6th March
item.description:
This van has been abandoned in this spot before. Ive tried reporting it the second time it has been abandoned and obviously my report was disreguarded. Im hoping now its been way over a month it has been abandoned there, the council may actually do something about it. It is almost impossible for most neighbours around this van to get out of their driveways and park. The van has been there since the start of february and it is very irritating. Especially considering the council will do nothing about this abandoned van as it is taxed. Just because it is taxed does not mean it is not abandoned. This is the second time this has happened now and we are fed up!!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hollingworth Road, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fridge fly tipped on footpath, 6th March
item.description:
Fridge fly tipped on footpath

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Quinton Close, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 3 blue sacks of litter for collection, 6th March
item.description:
Please collect 3 sacks of litter from the waterside gardens end of footpath 106, near Crayford Social Club

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crayford Way, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 6 blue bags of litter and scrap metal, 6th March
item.description:
Please collect 6 sacks of litter and some scrap metal from litter pick where footpath 106 meets Barnes Cray Road Crayford

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Barnes Cray Road, Barnes Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Domestic waste dump, 6th March
item.description:
The property on corner of Penge High Street has a bin storage cupboard in Crampton Road. The door does not open fully and the waste spills out. The room is never completely cleared. This is an ongoing issue that never gets fully resolved.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Crampton Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cones STILL not removed from outside 58 De Laune Street, 6th March
item.description:
I reported this problem on 28 February... your data base says 'fixed' but the traffic cones and tape are still there and there is no evidence of any work taking place.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 54, De Laune Street, Kennington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned waste, 6th March
item.description:
Rubbish has been left here for more than 2 weeks. The bin belongs to the Hemingway pub but is not used. People put their rubbish next to it and then stays and gets messier and more. This is not the first time, and it would be great if it could collected.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Skipworth Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cone removal, 6th March
item.description:
Cones have been left on the pavement outside 65 Pinewood Avenue, Sidcup. The work was carried out 3 to 4 weeks ago. Cones need to be collected as they have blown onto the road a couple of times
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48, Pinewood Avenue, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flyposting, 6th March
item.description:
Agent is advertising/posting a board on the Bromley Council payment at entrance to Brookwood Close.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brookwood Close, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping on corner, 6th March
item.description:
Large amount of fly tipping

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 43, Lyndhurst Grove, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping junction Brookedene and Hylton, 6th March
item.description:
As title, black sacks ripped by telephone box. Gaming chair at Hylton and sack of rubbish opposite side of road. Thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brookdene Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Young tree has been damaged in ground, 6th March
item.description:
Hello, It looks like someone has vandalised a young tree growing in front of my home on Park Road in the public grass area.The root is partly dug up and the tree is crooked as a result. Please can someone replant tree before it dies. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Two fridge freezers, broken glass, scooters and other rubbish, 6th March
item.description:
There are two fridge freezers, two scooters, broken glass on pavement and other rubbish generally discarded outside garage number 6 on Ancona Road. Really unsightly and dangerous in close proximity to a school with young children.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Walmer Terrace, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loose/raised/uneven paving stones, 6th March
item.description:
Large patch of loose uneven and raised paving. Trip hazard

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20a, Cathcart Hill, Archway, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on council owned garages, 6th March
item.description:
Graffiti on council owned garages - ideally want it removed swiftly so that it doesnt become a place where more and more graffiti is added

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sansom Street, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
